section.heading: Limit of weight of penalty mail; postage on overweight matter

subsection.chapeau: Penalty mail is restricted to articles not in excess of the weight and size prescribed for that class of mail receiving high priority in handling and delivery, except—

paragraph.content: stamped paper and supplies sold or used by the Postal Service; and
books and documents published or circulated by order of Congress when mailed by the Superintendent of Documents.

subsection.chapeau: A penalty mail article which is—

paragraph.content: not in excess of the weight and size prescribed for mail matter; and

subsection.continuation: is mailable at rates for that class of mail entitled to the lowest priority in handling and delivery, even though it may include written matter and may be sealed.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective July 1, 1971, pursuant to Resolution No. 71–9 of the Board of Governors. See section 15(a) of Pub. L. 91–375, set out as a note preceding section 101 of this title.
